pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh and export_result.sh; confirmed file path is where downloaded DHCP pcap is stored
Code analysis of setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py; confirmed as the target output file location
Code analysis of setup_task.sh and export_result.sh; confirmed as the hidden ground truth directory path
Downloaded and verified from official Wireshark Wiki (https://wiki.wireshark.org/SampleCaptures); file confirmed as official Wireshark sample capture
Direct analysis: tshark -r /tmp/dhcp.pcap -Y 'dhcp || bootp' | wc -l; file contains exactly 4 DHCP/BOOTP packets
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.id field shows two unique transaction IDs
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.hw.mac_addr shows single unique client MAC across all transactions
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.ip.your (Your IP/yiaddr) in DHCP Offer and ACK messages
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.option.dhcp_server_id (Option 54) across all DHCP messages
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.option.subnet_mask (Option 1) in Offer and ACK messages
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.option.router (Option 3) returns no results
Direct PCAP analysis: tshark field extraction of dhcp.option.domain_name_server (Option 6) returns no results
Confirmed on https://wiki.wireshark.org/SampleCaptures which lists dhcp.pcap as 'A sample of DHCP traffic'
Downloaded from official Wireshark wiki and verified to be a real sample capture file, not synthetically generated

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real DHCP packet capture file (dhcp.pcap) from the official Wireshark Sample Captures repository. The PCAP file contains 4 DHCP packets representing two complete DHCP lease transactions: Transaction 0x00003d1d (DHCP Discover → Offer) and Transaction 0x00003d1e (DHCP Request → ACK). Both transactions involve a single client with MAC address 00:0b:82:01:fc:42, which is assigned IP address 192.168.0.10 by server 192.168.0.1 with subnet mask 255.255.255.0. No router or DNS server options are present in this capture.
